proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_pembrolizumab (marginal main effect, all-comers).
Patients receiving treatment_sotorasib have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_sotorasib (marginal main effect, all-comers).
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_olaparib (marginal main effect, all-comers).
Patients receiving treatment_osimertinib have a different mean pfs_months than patients not receiving treatment_osimertinib (marginal main effect, all-comers).

analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab=1 mean pfs_months=3.414 (n=25008) vs =0 mean=3.449 (n=24992); two-sample t-test p=0.081. No significant marginal effect.
Sotorasib=1 mean pfs_months=3.641 (n=17544) vs =0 mean=3.319 (n=32456); diff +0.322 months, p=1.6e-53. Significant favorable marginal effect.
Olaparib=1 mean=3.414 vs =0 mean=3.439; diff -0.025 months, p=0.255. No significant marginal effect.
Osimertinib=1 mean=3.434 vs =0 mean=3.431; diff +0.004 months, p=0.870. No significant marginal effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Older age_years is associated with shorter pfs_months (negative correlation), reflecting typical worse prognosis with age.
Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with shorter pfs_months.
stage_iv=1 (metastatic disease) is associated with shorter pfs_months than stage_iv=0.
has_brain_mets=1 is associated with shorter pfs_months than has_brain_mets=0.
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with longer pfs_months.
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with shorter pfs_months.
Squamous histology is associated with shorter pfs_months than adenocarcinoma.
Current smokers have shorter pfs_months than former or never smokers.
sex_female=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than sex_female=0 patients.
Higher nlr and crp_mg_l are associated with shorter pfs_months.

analyses.result_summary: Pearson r=+0.757 (p<1e-300). Surprisingly, OLDER age is associated with LONGER pfs_months in this cohort. Bin means: 30-40y mean=0.08, 40-50y=0.60, 50-60y=1.79, 60-70y=3.37, 70-80y=5.03, 80-90y=6.91. Direction OPPOSITE of clinical expectation.
Mean PFS: ECOG 0=4.327 (n=17557), ECOG 1=3.185 (n=24962), ECOG 2=2.152 (n=7481). Strong monotonic decrease.
stage_iv=1 mean=2.925 vs =0 mean=4.362; diff -1.44 months, p<1e-300.
has_brain_mets=1 mean=2.743 vs =0 mean=3.663; diff -0.92 months, p<1e-300.
Pearson r=+0.099, p=2.8e-109. Higher albumin → longer PFS, as expected.
Pearson r=-0.127, p=7.5e-179. Greater weight loss → shorter PFS.
Pearson r=-0.0185, p=3.6e-5. Small but statistically significant negative association.
adenocarcinoma mean=3.670 (n=36099) vs squamous mean=2.813 (n=13901); diff -0.857 months for squamous, p<1e-300.
Means: current=2.987 (n=14974), former=3.607 (n=27567), never=3.675 (n=7459). One-way ANOVA F=433, p=3.7e-187. Current smokers have markedly worse PFS.
sex_female=1 mean=3.319 vs =0 mean=3.523; diff -0.204 months for females, p=2.4e-24.

analyses.code: Pearson r for nlr and crp_mg_l vs pfs
analyses.result_summary: NLR: r=+0.004, p=0.42 (NS). CRP: r=+0.001, p=0.78 (NS). Inflammation markers not associated with PFS in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: egfr_mutation=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than egfr_mutation=0 patients (marginal).
kras_g12c=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than kras_g12c=0 patients (marginal).
alk_fusion=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than alk_fusion=0 patients (marginal).
stk11_mutation=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than stk11_mutation=0 patients (marginal).
brca2_mutation=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than brca2_mutation=0 patients (marginal).
Higher pdl1_tps is associated with longer pfs_months.
tmb_high=1 patients have different mean pfs_months than tmb_high=0 patients.

analyses.result_summary: EGFR+ mean=3.596 (n=6517) vs EGFR- mean=3.407; diff +0.189, p=1.9e-10. Slightly favorable but small.
KRAS G12C+ mean=4.114 (n=6368) vs KRAS- mean=3.332; diff +0.782, p=4.8e-151. (Driven largely by sotorasib effect within KRAS+ — see iteration 4.)
ALK fusion+ mean=2.661 (n=2464) vs ALK- mean=3.472; diff -0.810 months, p=2.9e-69.
STK11+ mean=3.439 vs STK11- mean=3.430; diff +0.009, p=0.75. No marginal effect.
BRCA2+ mean=3.316 (n=1519) vs BRCA2- mean=3.435; diff -0.119, p=0.041. Marginally worse PFS.
Pearson r=+0.006, p=0.16. Essentially zero association — surprising given PDL1's role in immunotherapy biology.
TMB high+ mean=3.296 vs TMB- mean=3.486; diff -0.190 months for high TMB, p=9.1e-18. UNEXPECTED direction (high TMB usually favorable).

proposed_hypotheses.text: treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months more in patients with higher pdl1_tps than in those with lower pdl1_tps (positive treatment×pdl1_tps interaction).
treatment_pembrolizumab improves pfs_months more in tmb_high=1 patients than tmb_high=0 patients.
treatment_sotorasib improves pfs_months substantially more in kras_g12c=1 patients than kras_g12c=0 patients.
treatment_olaparib improves pfs_months more in brca2_mutation=1 patients than brca2_mutation=0 patients.
treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months more in egfr_mutation=1 patients than egfr_mutation=0 patients.
treatment_osimertinib improves pfs_months more in alk_fusion=1 patients than alk_fusion=0 patients (off-label/unexpected).

analyses.result_summary: Interaction coefficient = -0.116 (p=0.22). NO positive interaction; if anything slightly negative. Strata: at PDL1>=0.7 pembro -0.28 vs control (p=0.018, harm). REFUTED.
Interaction = -0.018 (p=0.685). REFUTED.

Interaction = +2.558 months (p=4e-294). Stratified means: KRAS+ sotorasib=1 mean 5.766 vs sotorasib=0 mean 3.213 (diff +2.55); KRAS- sotorasib=1 mean 3.329 vs =0 mean 3.334 (diff -0.005). STRONGLY SUPPORTED — primary actionable effect.
Interaction = +0.037 (p=0.78). Stratified BRCA2+: olaparib=1 mean 3.324 vs =0 mean 3.314 (diff +0.010, NS). REFUTED — no synthetic-lethal benefit observed.
Interaction = -0.020 (p=0.76). Stratified EGFR+: osimertinib=1 mean 3.586 vs =0 mean 3.600 (diff -0.014, NS). REFUTED — no targeted-therapy benefit despite EGFR mutation.

Interaction = +0.238 (p=0.017). Small significant interaction, but absolute effect within ALK fusion+ is osimertinib=1 mean 2.822 vs =0 mean 2.591 (diff +0.23 months) — modest and not clinically meaningful.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The age–pfs_months relationship is monotonically increasing across the age range (no inverted-U shape).
Each treatment is balanced across baseline covariates (age, ECOG, stage, brain mets, sex), suggesting random or RCT-like assignment rather than confounded EHR-driven assignment.

analyses.result_summary: Linear coef +0.016 (p=0.011) and quadratic +0.0012 (p<1e-100). Spearman r=+0.769. The relationship is monotonic-and-accelerating (convex) across 30-90y. SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Compare covariate means by each treatment
analyses.result_summary: Differences in mean age, ECOG, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, sex_female between treated and untreated for all four treatments are all <0.02 (absolute). Treatment assignment is essentially balanced — supports causal interpretation of within-stratum treatment effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within KRAS G12C+ patients, the sotorasib treatment effect on pfs_months is heterogeneous across baseline features (some features modify the effect).
Within KRAS G12C+, the sotorasib effect on pfs_months is markedly attenuated in sex_female=1 patients compared to sex_female=0 patients (negative treatment×sex interaction).
Within KRAS G12C+, the sotorasib effect on pfs_months is attenuated in alk_fusion=1 patients (negative treatment×alk interaction).
Within KRAS G12C+, the sotorasib effect on pfs_months is attenuated in brca2_mutation=1 patients (negative treatment×brca2 interaction).

analyses.result_summary: Top interactions (sorted by p): sex_female -4.66 (p<1e-300), alk_fusion -3.02 (p=2.9e-18), brca2_mutation -2.55 (p=2.3e-10), age_years +0.021 (p=2.7e-5), albumin -0.34 (p=0.012). Three biomarkers (sex_female, alk_fusion, brca2_mutation) each subtract roughly the entire sotorasib effect. SUPPORTED.

KRAS+ males: sotorasib=1 mean 7.876 (n=1237) vs =0 mean 3.232 (n=2271), diff +4.644. KRAS+ females: sotorasib=1 mean 3.179 (n=1009) vs =0 mean 3.191 (n=1851), diff -0.012 (t-test p=0.88). The sotorasib benefit is essentially erased in KRAS+ females. STRONGLY SUPPORTED.
Interaction -3.02, p=2.9e-18. KRAS+ male alk_fusion+ stratum: sotorasib=1 mean 2.354 vs =0 mean 2.639 (diff -0.285), n=138. The KRAS+/sotorasib benefit is also erased by ALK fusion. SUPPORTED.
Interaction -2.55, p=2.3e-10. KRAS+ male brca2+ stratum: sotorasib=1 mean 3.411 vs =0 mean 3.479 (diff -0.068), n=107. Sotorasib benefit erased in BRCA2+ as well. SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The 'clean' sotorasib responder subgroup is defined by kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND alk_fusion=0 AND brca2_mutation=0; in this subgroup, treatment_sotorasib increases mean pfs_months by ~5 months over no-sotorasib.
Presence of any one of {sex_female=1, alk_fusion=1, brca2_mutation=1} fully suppresses the sotorasib effect (mean PFS difference ~0) within KRAS G12C+ patients.

analyses.result_summary: n=3266 (sotorasib=1: 1154, =0: 2112). Mean PFS sotorasib=1: 8.234 vs sotorasib=0: 3.249. Diff +4.985, t-test p<1e-300. Robust across ECOG strata (+5.00, +5.08, +4.73 for ECOG 0/1/2) and across histology (+5.09 adenoca, +4.85 squamous). SUPPORTED — this is the primary actionable subgroup.
any_killer=0 (n=3266): sotorasib diff = +4.985. any_killer=1 (n=3102): sotorasib diff = -0.019 (NS). Each killer feature alone suffices to fully eliminate the benefit. SUPPORTED.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across binary biomarker pairs, treatment_pembrolizumab has at least one subgroup where it produces a meaningful pfs_months gain.
Across binary biomarker pairs, treatment_olaparib has at least one subgroup where it produces a meaningful pfs_months gain.
Across binary biomarker pairs, treatment_osimertinib has at least one subgroup where it produces a meaningful pfs_months gain.

analyses.code: Exhaustive 2-way subgroup screen (binary feature × binary feature) for treatment_pembrolizumab effect on pfs
analyses.result_summary: Out of >100 subgroups screened (all pairwise combinations of {sex_female, stage_iv, has_brain_mets, mutations, tmb_high, pdl1_high, histology, smoking, ecog}), no subgroup shows a pembrolizumab benefit >0.16 months. Top hits: sex_female & egfr_mutation +0.15 (p=0.05); stage_iv & ecog0 +0.06 (p=0.09). PDL1 quartile analysis: diffs -0.003, -0.027, -0.038, -0.072 (Q4 worst). Pembrolizumab provides no PFS benefit anywhere. REFUTED.

Top hits: egfr_mutation & stk11_mutation +0.33 (p=0.026, n=980); stk11_mutation & adeno +0.13 (p=0.04). Within BRCA2+ (n=1519): no effect (diff +0.010, p=0.93). No subgroup reproduces the kind of large effect seen with sotorasib. REFUTED — olaparib appears inert in this cohort.

Top hits: stage_iv & brca2_mutation -0.38 (p=0.007, n=976, harm); kras_g12c & alk_fusion +0.67 (p=0.034, n=259, very small subgroup); within EGFR+ subgroup variants: all NS, max +0.09. Within EGFR+ adenocarcinoma never-smokers: diff -0.009, p=0.90. REFUTED — osimertinib shows no clinically meaningful benefit anywhere.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for prognostic factors and including a 3-way treatment_sotorasib×kras_g12c×sex_female interaction plus treatment_sotorasib×alk_fusion and treatment_sotorasib×brca2_mutation interactions, the model explains the great majority of variance in pfs_months and confirms that the only treatment with a non-null PFS effect is sotorasib in the targeted subgroup.
In the multivariable model, treatment_pembrolizumab, treatment_olaparib, and treatment_osimertinib have no significant pfs_months effect (coefficients near zero).

analyses.code: smf.ols with all prognostic factors + treatment_sotorasib*kras_g12c*sex_female + alk/brca2 sotorasib interactions
analyses.result_summary: R²=0.977 on n=50000. Key adjusted coefficients: treatment_sotorasib (main) +0.022 (NS in non-KRAS); treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c +4.588; treatment_sotorasib:kras_g12c:sex_female -4.586; treatment_sotorasib:alk_fusion -0.264; treatment_sotorasib:brca2_mutation -0.367; main kras_g12c, alk_fusion, brca2_mutation, egfr_mutation, stk11, pdl1_tps, sex_female, nlr, crp_mg_l all NS after adjustment. Strong prognostic factors: age (+0.014 linear and +0.0012 quadratic), ECOG -1.10/level, stage_iv -1.42, brain_mets -0.91, squamous -0.73, current_smk -0.55 vs former, albumin +0.46/g·dL, weight_loss -0.073/%, ldh -0.0005/U·L. SUPPORTED.

Pembrolizumab: -0.0022 (p=0.46). Olaparib: +0.0033 (p=0.32). Osimertinib: +0.0017 (p=0.61). All effectively zero. SUPPORTED — these three treatments have no measurable pfs_months effect in this cohort.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is no joint subgroup defined by sex/ECOG/histology/smoking that rescues a meaningful treatment effect of osimertinib in egfr_mutation=1 patients, of olaparib in brca2_mutation=1 patients, or of pembrolizumab in pdl1_tps high patients.

The final, fully-specified treatment-effect subgroup with a clinically meaningful pfs_months gain is: kras_g12c=1 AND sex_female=0 AND alk_fusion=0 AND brca2_mutation=0; in this subgroup, treatment_sotorasib produces a +5 month gain in mean pfs_months versus no sotorasib.

analyses.code: OLS within EGFR+ ~ osimertinib*(sex_female+alk_fusion+brca2+stk11); OLS within BRCA2+ ~ olaparib*(sex_female+ecog_ps+has_brain_mets); pembro by PDL1 quartile and joint subgroups
analyses.result_summary: Within EGFR+ (n=6517): no significant osimertinib×modifier interactions (all p>0.08). Within BRCA2+ (n=1519): no significant olaparib×modifier interactions (all p>0.19). Pembrolizumab in PDL1 Q4 ≥0.5 with adenocarcinoma: diff -0.08 (NS); in TMB high+PDL1 high: diff +0.08 (NS); in never-smokers with PDL1 high: diff -0.02 (NS). No joint subgroup rescues a meaningful effect. SUPPORTED.

analyses.code: Final stratified t-test in {kras_g12c=1, sex_female=0, alk_fusion=0, brca2_mutation=0}
analyses.result_summary: n=3266; sotorasib=1 mean PFS 8.234 (SD 2.27, n=1154) vs sotorasib=0 mean 3.249 (SD 2.07, n=2112); diff +4.985 months, t-test p<1e-300. Effect is robust across ECOG and histology strata (+4.7 to +5.1 months). This is the final best-supported actionable treatment-effect subgroup. STRONGLY SUPPORTED.
